services.name: Citizenship by Investment
services.summary: Vanuatu's flagship citizenship program offers a second passport in as fast as 1–2 months through a government-contributed investment.
requirements: Proof of sufficient funds for investment

faqs.How fast can I get a passport?: The Vanuatu Citizenship by Investment program typically takes 1–2 months from application submission to passport issuance.
faqs.Do I need to live in Vanuatu?: No, there is no physical residency requirement. You can obtain full citizenship and passport without ever living in the country.
faqs.Can my family also apply?: Yes, you can include your spouse and dependent children under 18 in your application.

services.name: Residence Permits
services.summary: Long-term and permanent residence options for individuals looking to live, work, or retire in Vanuatu.
features: No minimum income requirement for retirees

faqs.What types of residence permits are avai: We offer work visas, retiree visas, and long-term residence permits.
faqs.Can I work on a residence permit?: Yes, certain residence permits allow you to work in Vanuatu.

services.summary: Reunite with your family in Vanuatu. We assist with dependent visas, spouse sponsorship, and parent immigration.

faq.q: How long does it take to get Vanuatu citizenship?
faq.a: The Vanuatu Citizenship by Investment program typically takes 1–2 months from application submission to passport issuance, making it one of the fastest citizenship programs in the world.

faq.q: Do I need to live in Vanuatu to get citizenship?
faq.a: No, Vanuatu's citizenship program has no physical residency requirement. You can obtain full citizenship and a passport without ever living in the country. There are also no language tests or interview requirements.

faq.q: Can I include my family in the application?
faq.a: Yes, you can include your spouse and dependent children under the age of 18 in your citizenship application. Additional fees apply for each dependent.

faq.q: What are the fees and costs involved?
faq.a: Costs vary by program. The citizenship by investment program includes a government contribution fee, application processing fees, due diligence fees, and professional service fees. Contact us for a detailed fee schedule tailored to your situation.

faq.q: What if my application is refused?
faq.a: Vanuatu has a straightforward due diligence process, and with proper documentation, the approval rate is very high. All applications we have submitted to date have been successful.

faq.q: Do I need to take a language test?
faq.a: No. Vanuatu has no language requirements, no education requirements, no age limits, and no interview process.

faq.q: Can I apply for citizenship remotely?
faq.a: Yes. The entire process can be completed remotely. You do not need to travel to Vanuatu at any point.
You typically need: a valid passport, birth certificate, police clearance certificate, medical certificate, passport photos, and proof of funds. Our team will guide you through the full list.

faq.q: How long is the Vanuatu passport valid?
faq.a: The Vanuatu passport is valid for 10 years for adults and can be renewed.
Vanuatu does not require you to renounce your existing citizenship. Dual citizenship is permitted.

faq.q: What countries can I visit visa-free with a Vanuatu passport?
faq.a: Vanuatu passport holders enjoy visa-free or visa-on-arrival access to 100+ countries including the UK, Hong Kong, Singapore, Russia, and Schengen Area countries.

faq.q: Can I work in Vanuatu after getting citizenship?
faq.a: Yes, citizens have full rights to live, work, and do business in Vanuatu without any restrictions.

faq.q: What is the minimum investment amount?
faq.a: The minimum government contribution for the Vanuatu Citizenship by Investment program starts at USD 130,000 for a single applicant. Contact us for current pricing.
Payment terms are structured around the application process. Contact our team for flexible payment options.

faq.q: How do I book a consultation?
faq.a: You can use the contact form on our website, email us at info@prioritycitizenship.vu, or call us at +678 12345. We typically respond within 24 hours.
Our office is located in Port Vila, Vanuatu at Rue de la République, PO Box 1234.
We are open Monday to Friday from 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM (Vanuatu Time), and Saturday from 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM.

faq.q: Do you offer services in Chinese?
faq.a: Yes, our team includes Mandarin-speaking consultants to assist Chinese clients throughout the application process.

faq.q: Is there an age limit for citizenship?
faq.a: There is no maximum age limit. Minors can be included in their parents' application.
